chunks.text: Physician Detection of Clinical Harm in Machine Translation: Quality Estimation Aids in Reliance and Backtranslation Identifies Critical Errors (EECS-2026-6) by Nikita Mehandru and Niloufar Salehi. This technical report explores how physicians can detect clinical harm in machine translation outputs. It examines the role of quality estimation in aiding reliance decisions and how backtranslation helps identify critical errors that could impact patient care.
chunks.question: What does the report on clinical harm in machine translation focus on?
chunks.answer: Physician detection of errors in medical translations.

chunks.text: Generalizable and Scalable Robot Learning in the Physical World (EECS-2026-4) by Fangchen Liu. This research addresses challenges in developing robot learning systems that are both generalizable across different environments and scalable for real-world physical applications. It likely discusses methods to improve adaptability and efficiency in robotic tasks.
chunks.question: What is the goal of the robot learning research?
chunks.answer: Creating adaptable and scalable physical robot systems.

chunks.text: Collaborative Language Agents (EECS-2026-2) by Jessy Lin. This technical report investigates collaborative language agents, focusing on how multiple AI agents can work together on language-based tasks. It may cover coordination mechanisms, communication protocols, and applications in areas like dialogue systems or text generation.
chunks.question: What do collaborative language agents involve?
chunks.answer: Multiple AI agents working together on language tasks.

chunks.text: Indoor Asset Detection in Large Scale 360° Drone-Captured Imagery via 3D Gaussian Splatting (EECS-2026-1) by Monica Tang. This report presents a method for detecting indoor assets using drone-captured 360-degree imagery. It employs 3D Gaussian splatting techniques to enhance accuracy and efficiency in identifying objects within large-scale indoor environments.
chunks.question: How does the indoor asset detection method work?
chunks.answer: Uses 3D Gaussian splatting on drone imagery.

chunks.text: BlendServe: Optimizing Offline Inference with Resource-Aware Batching by Yilong Zhao and Ion Stoica. This technical report introduces BlendServe, a system designed to optimize offline inference processes. It focuses on resource-aware batching strategies to improve computational efficiency and performance in machine learning inference tasks.
chunks.question: What does BlendServe optimize?
chunks.answer: Offline inference with efficient batching strategies.
